diagnostics.message: Project uses a motion library but has no prefers-reduced-motion handling ΓÇö required for accessibility (WCAG 2.3.3)
diagnostics.help: Add `useReducedMotion()` from your animation library, or a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` CSS query

diagnostics.title: Full Framer Motion import
diagnostics.message: Importing "motion" ships about 30 kb of extra code to your users & slows page load. Use "m" with LazyMotion instead.
diagnostics.help: Use `import { LazyMotion, m } from "framer-motion"` with `domAnimation` features. Saves about 30kb.

diagnostics.title: Static value rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `variants` inside `Badge` uses no local state but is rebuilt every render, so it looks new each time & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the value above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted and makes it look new every time.

diagnostics.title: Full Framer Motion import
diagnostics.message: Importing "motion" ships about 30 kb of extra code to your users & slows page load. Use "m" with LazyMotion instead.
diagnostics.help: Use `import { LazyMotion, m } from "framer-motion"` with `domAnimation` features. Saves about 30kb.

diagnostics.title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
diagnostics.message: "recharts" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
diagnostics.help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

diagnostics.title: Full Framer Motion import

diagnostics.title: Deprecated React 19 APIs
diagnostics.message: forwardRef is dead weight in React 19, since ref is a normal prop now, so drop it & pass ref straight through.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Button missing explicit type
diagnostics.message: Your users can submit the form by accident because a `<button>` with no `type` defaults to submit.
diagnostics.help: Always set a `type` on a `<button>`: `type="button"`, `"submit"`, or `"reset"`.

diagnostics.help: Give every interactive control a label screen readers can read.

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "i".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Label missing associated control
diagnostics.message: Blind users can't identify this field because screen readers find no label text, so add visible text, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
diagnostics.help: Tie every label to a control with `htmlFor`, or by nesting the input.

diagnostics.title: Control missing accessible label
diagnostics.message: Blind users can't tell what this control does because screen readers find no label, so add visible text, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
diagnostics.help: Give every interactive control a label screen readers can read.

diagnostics.title: Label missing associated control
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users can't tell which input this label names because it's tied to none, so add `htmlFor` or wrap the input inside it.
diagnostics.help: Tie every label to a control with `htmlFor`, or by nesting the input.

diagnostics.title: Click handler missing keyboard handler
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can't trigger this click handler because there's no keyboard one, so add `onKeyUp`, `onKeyDown`, or `onKeyPress`.
diagnostics.help: Pair `onClick` with a key handler so keyboard users can trigger it.

diagnostics.title: Interaction on static element
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users can't tell this click handler is interactive because it has no `role`, so add a `role` or use a button or link.
diagnostics.help: Give clickable static elements a `role`, or use a button or link.

diagnostics.title: Click handler missing keyboard handler
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can't trigger this click handler because there's no keyboard one, so add `onKeyUp`, `onKeyDown`, or `onKeyPress`.
diagnostics.help: Pair `onClick` with a key handler so keyboard users can trigger it.

diagnostics.title: Interaction on static element
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users can't tell this click handler is interactive because it has no `role`, so add a `role` or use a button or link.
diagnostics.help: Give clickable static elements a `role`, or use a button or link.

diagnostics.title: Full Framer Motion import
diagnostics.message: Importing "motion" ships about 30 kb of extra code to your users & slows page load. Use "m" with LazyMotion instead.
diagnostics.help: Use `import { LazyMotion, m } from "framer-motion"` with `domAnimation` features. Saves about 30kb.

diagnostics.title: Button missing explicit type
diagnostics.message: Your users can submit the form by accident because a `<button>` with no `type` defaults to submit.
diagnostics.help: Always set a `type` on a `<button>`: `type="button"`, `"submit"`, or `"reset"`.

diagnostics.message: Unused file ΓÇö not reachable from any entry point
diagnostics.help: Delete the file if it is truly unreachable, or import it from an entry point.

diagnostics.message: Unused dependency: `es-toolkit`
diagnostics.help: Remove the dependency from package.json if it is genuinely unused.

diagnostics.message: Unused devDependency: `react-doctor`
diagnostics.help: Remove the dependency from package.json if it is genuinely unused.
